page.title: Custom web app vs SaaS tool | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Custom web app vs SaaS tool: which makes more sense
page.description: A practical comparison of custom web application development and SaaS tools for companies evaluating internal systems, workflow-heavy software, or client portals.
page.primaryQuery: custom web app vs saas tool

hero.title: The real question is where the compromise will be cheaper
hero.subtitle: SaaS is sometimes the right choice. In other cases it only postpones the problem and makes the workflow harder over time.

intro: SaaS usually means faster start and lower upfront commitment. Custom development makes more sense when the generic tool would force the company to distort an important business process.
This is not only a technology decision. It is mainly an operational and economic one.

sections.title: When SaaS is the better option
body: SaaS is a good fit when the process is fairly standard, the team needs to move quickly, and the tool covers most needs without expensive workarounds.

sections.title: When custom development wins
body: Custom development is stronger when the company needs unique workflow logic, multiple roles, integrations, or long-term control over software that matters to operations.

sections.title: How to decide
body: The decision should not be based only on price or technology taste. What matters is operational fit, change speed, and long-term cost.

sections.title: Practical conclusion
body: If the software directly supports a business-critical process, custom development is often more sensible than spending years around SaaS limitations. If the need is standard and speed matters most, SaaS may be the right starting point.

faq.question: Is SaaS always cheaper?
faq.answer: No. The entry cost is often lower, but long-term cost can rise through manual workarounds, add-ons, integration complexity, and product constraints.

faq.question: Does it make sense to start with SaaS and move later?
faq.answer: Sometimes yes, but it helps to understand migration cost and lock-in risk before using that path as the default answer.

faq.question: When is custom development too early?
faq.answer: When the process is still unclear and a standard tool would cover most of the real work without painful compromise.

faq.question: Can companies combine SaaS and custom software?
faq.answer: Yes. Many practical setups keep commodity functions in SaaS and build custom logic around the parts that are genuinely differentiating.

notFor: abstract technology debates with no business context
